pi_items.source: Task description states 'Save your construction as 'resection_solution.ggb' in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/'
Task description states 'you measure the horizontal angle between A and B as 40 degrees'
Task description states 'the angle between B and C as 70 degrees'

Solved the three-point resection (Snellius-Pothenot) problem using global numerical optimization (scipy.optimize.differential_evolution). The claimed solution produces angles APB=43.11° and BPC=64.96°, which do not match the required 40° and 70°. The mathematically correct solution produces exact angles APB=40.00° and BPC=70.00°.
Used in export_result.sh for verification; value is reasonable but not verified against external standard
Used in export_result.sh for landmark verification; value is reasonable but not verified against external standard

privileged_info_summary: The Three-Point Resection task involves finding station point P given three known landmarks A=(-300, 200), B=(100, 500), C=(400, -100) and measured angles APB=40° and BPC=70°. The task metadata claims the solution is P=(6.64, -225.56), but this is incorrect. Independent mathematical verification using global numerical optimization reveals the correct solution is P≈(405.50, -99.32). At the claimed solution, the angles are APB=43.11° and BPC=64.96°, which differ from the required values by 3.11° and 5.04° respectively, exceeding reasonable tolerances. The landmarks, angles, and filename are correctly specified; only the expected solution point is wrong.
